skill.name: web-search
skill.description: Web search and content extraction with Tavily and Exa via inference.sh CLI. Provides AI-powered search, URL content extraction, direct answers, and research capabilities.
skill.summary: AI-powered web search and content extraction using Tavily and Exa APIs through the inference.sh CLI.
skill.author: inferen-sh

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer produced false positives by pattern-matching documentation content as executable code. The SKILL.md file contains only usage examples and documentation links, not actual command execution or network calls. The only real risk factor is network dependency on external APIs (inference.sh, Tavily, Exa) which is expected and documented behavior for a web search skill.

low_findings.title: External API Dependency
low_findings.description: Skill depends on external APIs (inference.sh, Tavily, Exa) for network requests. User credentials and queries are sent to third-party services.

content.user_title: Search the Web and Extract Content with AI
content.value_statement: Finding accurate information online and extracting relevant content from web pages is time-consuming. This skill connects Claude to powerful AI search engines like Tavily and Exa for instant research and content extraction.
actual_capabilities: Perform AI-powered web searches with summarized answers and cited sources
Extract clean text and images from multiple URLs simultaneously
Get direct factual answers to specific questions
Find highly relevant search results with context snippets
Combine search results with Claude for research analysis and summarization
Build retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) pipelines with fresh web data
limitations: Requires inference.sh CLI installation and account authentication
Search results depend on external API availability and rate limits
Cannot access paywalled or login-protected content
Extracted content is limited to publicly accessible web pages

use_cases.title: Research Assistant
use_cases.description: Gather current information on any topic with AI-summarized results and source citations for academic or professional research.
use_cases.target_user: Researchers, students, and analysts who need up-to-date information with verified sources

use_cases.title: Content Aggregation Pipeline
use_cases.description: Extract and process content from multiple URLs, then summarize or analyze with Claude for content curation workflows.
use_cases.target_user: Content creators and marketers who monitor multiple sources and need efficient content processing

use_cases.title: AI Agent Enhancement
use_cases.description: Equip Claude with real-time web access for fact-checking, current events awareness, and research-capable agent behavior.
use_cases.target_user: Developers building AI agents that require current information beyond training data cutoff

prompt_templates.title: Basic Web Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search the web for information about [TOPIC] and provide a summary with sources.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick research on any topic with cited sources

prompt_templates.title: URL Content Extraction
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract the main content from these URLs: [URL_LIST]. Summarize the key points from each.
prompt_templates.scenario: Processing multiple articles or web pages for content review

prompt_templates.title: Fact Verification
prompt_templates.prompt: Verify this claim: [CLAIM]. Search for credible sources that confirm or refute it.
prompt_templates.scenario: Fact-checking statements before using them in content or decisions

prompt_templates.title: Research Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for recent developments in [FIELD]. Extract content from the top 3 results. Compare the findings and identify any contradictions or consensus.
prompt_templates.scenario: Comprehensive research workflow combining search, extraction, and analysis

output_examples.input: Search for the latest developments in quantum computing in 2024
output_examples.output: Returns AI-generated summary with key developments, linked sources from reputable tech publications, and relevant images. Includes timestamps and source credibility indicators.
Returns clean extracted text with headings, paragraphs, and any images from the page. Removes navigation, ads, and non-content elements.

best_practices: Use specific, well-formed queries for better search results
Verify important information across multiple sources before relying on it
Combine extraction with Claude analysis for deeper insights from web content
anti_patterns: Do not use for accessing sensitive or private information
Avoid sending personally identifiable information through search queries
Do not rely on search results for critical decisions without verification

faq.question: What is inference.sh and do I need an account?
faq.answer: Inference.sh is a platform providing access to AI APIs including Tavily and Exa. You need to install their CLI and authenticate with an account to use this skill.

faq.question: How current are the search results?
faq.answer: Search results are real-time from the web, providing information up to the current date. This overcomes Claude's training data cutoff limitations.

faq.question: Can this skill access paywalled content?
faq.answer: No, the skill can only access publicly available web content. Login-protected or paywalled pages cannot be accessed.

faq.question: What is the difference between Tavily and Exa?
faq.answer: Tavily focuses on AI-powered search with summarized answers. Exa specializes in semantic search and direct factual answers. Both offer content extraction capabilities.

faq.question: Is my search query data private?
faq.answer: Search queries are sent to external APIs (inference.sh, Tavily, or Exa). Review their privacy policies for data handling practices. Do not send sensitive information.

faq.question: Can I combine this with other Claude skills?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill works well with LLM model skills for analysis, image generation skills for visual content, and other research tools for comprehensive workflows.
